heading: Adoption and effect of national standards
label: 30307 Adoption and effect of national standards
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 15607 of Title 42

children.heading: Final rule
children.label: 1 Final rule
children.text: Not later than 1 year after receiving the report specified in , the Attorney General shall publish a final rule adopting national standards for the detection, prevention, reduction, and punishment of prison rape.section 30306(d)(3) of this title

children.heading: Independent judgment
children.label: 2 Independent judgment
children.text: The standards referred to in paragraph (1) shall be based upon the independent judgment of the Attorney General, after giving due consideration to the recommended national standards provided by the Commission under , and being informed by such data, opinions, and proposals that the Attorney General determines to be appropriate to consider.section 30306(e) of this title

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 3 Limitation
children.text: The Attorney General shall not establish a national standard under this section that would impose substantial additional costs compared to the costs presently expended by Federal, State, and local prison authorities. The Attorney General may, however, provide a list of improvements for consideration by correctional facilities.

children.heading: Transmission to States
children.label: 4 Transmission to States
children.text: Within 90 days of publishing the final rule under paragraph (1), the Attorney General shall transmit the national standards adopted under such paragraph to the chief executive of each State, the head of the department of corrections of each State, and to the appropriate authorities in those units of local government who oversee operations in one or more prisons.

children.heading: Applicability to Federal Bureau of Prisons
children.label: b Applicability to Federal Bureau of Prisons
children.text: The national standards referred to in subsection (a) shall apply to the Federal Bureau of Prisons immediately upon adoption of the final rule under subsection (a)(4).

children.heading: Applicability to detention facilities operated by the Department of Homeland Security
children.label: c Applicability to detention facilities operated by the Department of Homeland Security

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary of Homeland Security shall publish a final rule adopting national standards for the detection, prevention, reduction, and punishment of rape and sexual assault in facilities that maintain custody of aliens detained for a violation of the immigrations laws of the United States.March 7, 2013

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: 2 Applicability
children.text: The standards adopted under paragraph (1) shall apply to detention facilities operated by the Department of Homeland Security and to detention facilities operated under contract with the Department.

children.label: A
children.text: assess compliance with the standards adopted under paragraph (1) on a regular basis; and

children.label: B
children.text: include the results of the assessments in performance evaluations of facilities completed by the Department of Homeland Security.

children.heading: Considerations
children.label: 4 Considerations
children.text: In adopting standards under paragraph (1), the Secretary of Homeland Security shall give due consideration to the recommended national standards provided by the Commission under .section 30306(e) of this title

children.heading: Definition
children.label: 5 Definition
children.text: As used in this section, the term “detention facilities operated under contract with the Department” includes, but is not limited to contract detention facilities and detention facilities operated through an intergovernmental service agreement with the Department of Homeland Security.

children.heading: Applicability to custodial facilities operated by the Department of Health and Human Services
children.label: d Applicability to custodial facilities operated by the Department of Health and Human Services

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary of Health and Human Services shall publish a final rule adopting national standards for the detection, prevention, reduction, and punishment of rape and sexual assault in facilities that maintain custody of unaccompanied alien children (as defined in ).March 7, 2013section 279(g) of title 6

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: 2 Applicability
children.text: The standards adopted under paragraph (1) shall apply to facilities operated by the Department of Health and Human Services and to facilities operated under contract with the Department.

children.label: A
children.text: assess compliance with the standards adopted under paragraph (1) on a regular basis; and

children.label: B
children.text: include the results of the assessments in performance evaluations of facilities completed by the Department of Health and Human Services.

children.heading: Considerations
children.label: 4 Considerations
children.text: In adopting standards under paragraph (1), the Secretary of Health and Human Services shall give due consideration to the recommended national standards provided by the Commission under .section 30306(e) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the program is carried out by or under the authority of the Attorney General;

children.label: ii
children.text: the program may provide amounts to States for prison purposes; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the program is not administered by the Office on Violence Against Women of the Department of Justice.

children.heading: List
children.label: B List
children.text: For each fiscal year, the Attorney General shall prepare a list identifying each program that meets the criteria of subparagraph (A) and provide that list to each State.

children.label: i
children.text: a certification that the State has adopted, and is in full compliance with, the national standards described in subsection (a); or

children.label: I
children.text: a commitment that not less than 5 percent of such amount shall be used for this purpose; or

children.label: II
children.text: a request that the Attorney General hold 5 percent of such amount in abeyance pursuant to the requirements of subparagraph (E).

children.label: I
children.text: a list of the prisons under the operational control of the executive branch of the State;

children.label: II
children.text: a list of the prisons listed under subclause (I) that were audited during the most recently concluded audit year;

children.label: III
children.text: all final audit reports for prisons listed under subclause (I) that were completed during the most recently concluded audit year; and

children.label: IV
children.text: a proposed schedule for completing an audit of all the prisons listed under subclause (I) during the following 3 audit years.

children.heading: Audit appeal exception
children.label: ii Audit appeal exception
children.text: Beginning on the date that is 3 years after , a chief executive officer of a State may submit a certification that the State is in full compliance pursuant to subparagraph (A)(i) even if a prison under the operational control of the executive branch of the State has an audit appeal pending.December 16, 2016

children.label: I
children.text: a list of the prisons under the operational control of the executive branch of the State;

children.label: II
children.text: a list of the prisons listed under subclause (I) that were audited during the most recently concluded audit year;

children.label: III
children.text: an explanation of any barriers the State faces to completing required audits;

children.label: IV
children.text: all final audit reports for prisons listed under subclause (I) that were completed during the most recently concluded audit year;

children.label: V
children.text: a proposed schedule for completing an audit of all prisons under the operational control of the executive branch of the State during the following 3 audit years; and

children.label: VI
children.text: an explanation of the State’s current degree of implementation of the national standards.

children.heading: Additional requirement
children.label: ii Additional requirement
children.text: A chief executive officer of a State who submits an assurance under subparagraph (A)(ii)(I) shall, before receiving the applicable funds described in subparagraph (A)(ii)(I), also provide the Attorney General with a proposed plan for the expenditure of the funds during the applicable grant period.

children.heading: Accounting of funds
children.label: iii Accounting of funds
children.text: A chief executive officer of a State who submits an assurance under subparagraph (A)(ii)(I) shall, in a manner consistent with the applicable grant reporting requirements, submit to the Attorney General a detailed accounting of how the funds described in subparagraph (A) were used.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: On the date that is 3 years after , subclause (II) of subparagraph (A)(ii) shall cease to have effect.December 16, 2016

children.heading: Additional sunset
children.label: ii Additional sunset
children.text: On the date that is 6 years after , clause (ii) of subparagraph (A) shall cease to have effect.December 16, 2016

children.heading: Request
children.label: I Request
children.text: Notwithstanding clause (ii), during the 2-year period beginning 6 years after , a chief executive officer of a State who certifies that the State has audited not less than 90 percent of prisons under the operational control of the executive branch of the State may request that the Attorney General allow the chief executive officer to submit an emergency assurance in accordance with subparagraph (A)(ii) as in effect on the day before the date on which that subparagraph ceased to have effect under clause (ii) of this subparagraph.December 16, 2016

children.heading: Grant of request
children.label: II Grant of request
children.text: The Attorney General shall grant a request submitted under subclause (I) within 60 days upon a showing of good cause.

children.heading: Disposition of funds held in abeyance
children.label: E Disposition of funds held in abeyance

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: If the chief executive officer of a State who has submitted an assurance under subparagraph (A)(ii)(II) subsequently submits a certification under subparagraph (A)(i) during the 3-year period beginning on , the Attorney General will release all funds held in abeyance under subparagraph (A)(ii)(II) to be used by the State in accordance with the conditions of the grant program for which the funds were provided.December 16, 2016

children.heading: Release of funds
children.label: ii Release of funds
children.text: If the chief executive officer of a State who has submitted an assurance under subparagraph (A)(ii)(II) is unable to submit a certification during the 3-year period beginning on , but does assure the Attorney General that ⅔ of prisons under the operational control of the executive branch of the State have been audited at least once, the Attorney General shall release all of the funds of the State held in abeyance to be used in adopting and achieving full compliance with the national standards, if the State agrees to comply with the applicable requirements in clauses (ii) and (iii) of subparagraph (C).December 16, 2016

children.heading: Redistribution of funds
children.label: iii Redistribution of funds
children.text: If the chief executive officer of a State who has submitted an assurance under subparagraph (A)(ii)(II) is unable to submit a certification during the 3-year period beginning on , and does not assure the Attorney General that ⅔ of prisons under the operational control of the executive branch of the State have been audited at least once, the Attorney General shall redistribute the funds of the State held in abeyance to other States to be used in accordance with the conditions of the grant program for which the funds were provided.December 16, 2016

children.heading: Publication of audit results
children.label: F Publication of audit results
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Attorney General shall request from each State, and make available on an appropriate Internet website, all final audit reports completed to date for prisons under the operational control of the executive branch of each State. The Attorney General shall update such website annually with reports received from States under subparagraphs (B)(i) and (C)(i).December 16, 2016

children.heading: Report on implementation of national standards
children.label: G Report on implementation of national standards
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Attorney General shall issue a report to the Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate and the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives on the status of implementation of the national standards and the steps the Department, in conjunction with the States and other key stakeholders, is taking to address any unresolved implementation issues.December 16, 2016

children.heading: Report on noncompliance
children.label: 3 Report on noncompliance
children.text: Not later than September 30 of each year, the Attorney General shall publish a report listing each grantee that is not in compliance with the national standards adopted pursuant to subsection (a).

children.heading: Cooperation with survey
children.label: 4 Cooperation with survey
children.text: For each fiscal year, any amount that a State receives for that fiscal year under a grant program covered by this subsection shall not be used for prison purposes (and shall be returned to the grant program if no other authorized use is available), unless the chief executive of the State submits to the Attorney General a certification that neither the State, nor any political subdivision or unit of local government within the State, is listed in a report issued by the Attorney General pursuant to .section 30303(c)(2)(C) of this title

children.heading: Redistribution of amounts
children.label: 5 Redistribution of amounts
children.text: Amounts under a grant program not granted by reason of a reduction under paragraph (2), or returned by reason of the prohibition in paragraph (4), shall be granted to one or more entities not subject to such reduction or such prohibition, subject to the other laws governing that program.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: 6 Implementation
children.text: The Attorney General shall establish procedures to implement this subsection, including procedures for effectively applying this subsection to discretionary grant programs.

children.heading: Requirement of adoption of standards
children.label: A Requirement of adoption of standards
children.text: The first grants to which paragraph (2) applies are grants for the second fiscal year beginning after the date on which the national standards under subsection (a) are finalized.

children.heading: Requirement for cooperation
children.label: B Requirement for cooperation
children.text: The first grants to which paragraph (4) applies are grants for the fiscal year beginning after .September 4, 2003

children.heading: Background checks for auditors
children.label: i Background checks for auditors
children.text: An individual seeking certification by the Department of Justice to serve as an auditor of prison compliance with the national standards described in subsection (a) shall, upon request, submit fingerprints in the manner determined by the Attorney General for criminal history record checks of the applicable State and Federal Bureau of Investigation repositories.

children.heading: Certification agreements
children.label: ii Certification agreements
children.text: Each auditor certified under this paragraph shall sign a certification agreement that includes the provisions of, or provisions that are substantially similar to, the Bureau of Justice Assistance’s Auditor Certification Agreement in use in April 2018.

children.heading: Auditor evaluation
children.label: iii Auditor evaluation
children.text: The PREA Management Office of the Bureau of Justice Assistance shall evaluate all auditors based on the criteria contained in the certification agreement. In the case that an auditor fails to comply with a certification agreement or to conduct audits in accordance with the PREA Auditor Handbook, audit methodology, and instrument approved by the PREA Management Office, the Office may take remedial or disciplinary action, as appropriate, including decertifying the auditor in accordance with subparagraph (B).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The PREA Management Office may suspend an auditor’s certification during an evaluation of an auditor’s performance under subparagraph (A)(iii). The PREA Management Office shall promptly publish the names of auditors who have been decertified, and the reason for decertification. Auditors who have been decertified or are on suspension may not participate in audits described in subsection (a), including as an agent of a certified auditor.

children.heading: Notification
children.label: ii Notification
children.text: In the case that an auditor is decertified, the PREA Management Office shall inform each facility or agency at which the auditor performed an audit during the relevant 3-year audit cycle, and may recommend that the agency repeat any affected audits, if appropriate.

children.heading: Audit assignments
children.label: C Audit assignments
children.text: The PREA Management Office shall establish a system, to be administered by the Office, for assigning certified auditors to Federal, State, and local facilities.

children.heading: Disclosure of documentation
children.label: D Disclosure of documentation
children.text: The Director of the Bureau of Prisons shall comply with each request for documentation necessary to conduct an audit under subsection (a), which is made by a certified auditor in accordance with the provisions of the certification agreement described in subparagraph (A)(ii). The Director of the Bureau of Prisons may require an auditor to sign a confidentiality agreement or other agreement designed to address the auditor’s use of personally identifiable information, except that such an agreement may not limit an auditor’s ability to provide all such documentation to the Department of Justice, as required under section 115.401(j) of title 28, Code of Federal Regulations.
